etymology.description: 彳(“walk”) + 止(“foot” or “to stop”) = walking and stopping actions combined
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 彳(chì, "walk") representing movement and 止(zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop") to depict the action of walking by emphasizing continuous movement and pauses, fitting the concept of 'walk'. The modern form is stylized with 龰, preserving the original idea of movement.

examples.chinese: 在古代，中国人用脚步来计量距离(在古代，中國人用腳步來計量距離)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài, Zhōngguó rén yòng jiǎobù lái jìliàng jùlí.
examples.english: In ancient times, Chinese people used footsteps to measure distances.

examples.chinese: 这条小径通向村庄(這條小徑通向村莊)，人们常沿着它步行(人們常沿着它步行)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo xiǎojìng tōng xiàng cūnzhuāng, rénmen cháng yánzhe tā bùxíng.
examples.english: This path leads to the village, and people often walk along it.

examples.chinese: 他每天步行到学校(他每天步行到學校)，享受沿途的风景(享受沿途的風景)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān bùxíng dào xuéxiào, xiǎngshòu yántú de fēngjǐng.
examples.english: He walks to school every day, enjoying the scenery along the way.
